title: 10 Best Herbal Remedies for Altitude Sickness

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Quercetin is good for altitude sickness because it helps to prevent the blood vessels from getting too dilated, which can lead to headaches and other symptoms of altitude sickness.

Ginkgolide B is good for altitude sickness because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce swelling in the brain caused by high altitudes, thus preventing conditions such as high-altitude pulmonary edema.

Bilobalide is good for altitude sickness because it helps to widen blood vessels and improve blood flow to the brain, which can reduce the symptoms of altitude sickness such as headaches and dizziness.
Isorhapontigenin is good for altitude sickness because it helps to reduce the inflammation in the brain that can lead to altitude sickness, thereby preventing the condition from getting worse.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba creams are sometimes used to help with altitude sickness. This sickness happens when people travel to high places and their bodies don't get enough oxygen. Ginkgo biloba is a plant that has been used for a long time to help improve blood flow and increase oxygen to the brain. When applied to the skin in a cream form, it may help improve blood flow to the body's tissues. This could be beneficial for people with altitude sickness, as their bodies need more oxygen to function properly. Some people believe that Ginkgo biloba creams can help reduce the symptoms of altitude sickness, such as headaches and fatigue. However, more research is needed to confirm this.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Angelica archangelica herbal cream for altitude sickness, start by gathering dried Angelica root and leaves, which can be bought from a health food store or grown in your garden. First, combine 2 cups of dried Angelica with 4 cups of boiling water in a large pot, and let it steep for 10-15 minutes to create a strong tea. After the steeping time is over, strain the tea through a cheesecloth or a fine mesh into a clean bowl, and discard the solids. Next, combine 1 cup of the cooled tea with 1/2 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, and 2 tablespoons of shea butter in a small saucepan. Heat the mixture over low heat, stirring constantly, until the beeswax and shea butter have melted. Remove the saucepan from the heat and let it cool slightly before pouring the mixture into small glass jars. As the mixture cools and solidifies, it will thicken into a rich and nourishing cream that can be applied to the skin to help alleviate the symptoms of altitude sickness, such as headaches and fatigue.
remedies.image_alt: Ginkgo biloba herbal creams for altitude sickness

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Gingerol is good for altitude sickness because it helps to increase oxygen levels in the body, reduce inflammation, and improve blood flow to the brain, which can alleviate symptoms such as headaches and dizziness.

Shogaol is good for altitude sickness because it can help to reduce inflammation in the body and improve blood flow to the brain, which can help to alleviate the symptoms of altitude sickness such as headaches and fatigue.

Zingiberene is good for altitude sickness because it helps to lower blood pressure and improve circulation, which can reduce the risk of dehydration and other symptoms that occur when the body adapts to high altitudes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale, also known as ginger, is a natural remedy that can help with altitude sickness. When you go to high places, your body needs to get used to the lower air pressure. This can cause headaches, nausea, and fatigue. Ginger has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and pain in the body. It also has a compound called gingerol, which can help reduce inflammation in the stomach and ease nausea. When you take ginger in the form of cream, the active ingredients can be absorbed into your body quickly, providing fast relief from symptoms of altitude sickness. The cream can be applied directly to the skin, or taken orally, making it a convenient and effective treatment option.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Angelica archangelica herbal cream that helps with altitude sickness, you will first need to gather a few ingredients. Start by drying Angelica archangelica roots and leaves, either by air-drying them or using a dehydrator. Then, combine 1 part of dried Angelica archangelica with 2 parts of carrier oil, such as coconut or olive oil, in a clean glass jar. Let the mixture infuse in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar occasionally, to allow the Angelica's active compounds to transfer into the oil. After the infusion period, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar. Next, add a small amount of beeswax and a natural emollient, such as shea butter or cocoa butter, to the infused oil. Place the jar in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water, and gently heat the mixture until the beeswax has melted and the ingredients are well combined. Remove the jar from the heat, let it cool slightly, and then pour the mixture into small tin containers. As the mixture cools and solidifies, it will become a rich, creamy herbal cream that can be applied to the skin to help alleviate symptoms of altitude sickness, such as headaches and fatigue.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal creams for altitude sickness

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Polyphenols are good for altitude sickness because they can help reduce inflammation and improve blood flow to the brain, which can alleviate symptoms such as headaches and dizziness that occur when the body adjusts to high altitudes.

Ginsenosides are good for altitude sickness because they help to increase blood flow and reduce inflammation in the body, which can help to alleviate symptoms such as headaches and fatigue that are associated with high altitude.

Saponins are good for altitude sickness because they help reduce swelling in the blood vessels and improve blood flow to the brain, which can be helpful in preventing the headaches and other symptoms associated with high altitudes.

Ginsenoside Rb1 is good for altitude sickness because it helps to increase blood flow to the brain and improve oxygen delivery, which can help to reduce the symptoms of altitude sickness such as headaches and fatigue.

remedies.remedy_desc: Panax ginseng creams can help with altitude sickness because it has special powers that help the body adapt to high elevations. The cream contains ginsenosides, which are chemicals that make the blood vessels stronger and more flexible. This is important because at high altitudes, the air is thinner and there is less oxygen. When the blood vessels are strong, they can carry more oxygen to the body's cells, making it easier to breathe and feel comfortable. Panax ginseng creams also help reduce inflammation and improve blood flow, which can reduce the symptoms of altitude sickness such as headaches and fatigue. By using a cream with Panax ginseng, people can help their body cope with the changes that happen at high elevations and feel better faster.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Angelica archangelica herbal creams that can help with altitude sickness, start by gathering a few ingredients. You'll need dried Angelica root, coconut oil, beeswax, and a carrier oil like jojoba or sweet almond oil. Begin by infusing the Angelica root in a carrier oil, such as jojoba or sweet almond oil, in a small saucepan over low heat for a few hours or by letting it sit for a few days in a cool, dark place. Once the infusion is complete, strain the liquid and discard the solids. Next, combine the infused oil with coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water. Stir the mixture until the beeswax has melted, then remove the bowl from the heat and let it cool until it reaches a consistency suitable for applying to the skin. Pour the mixture into small containers, let it cool and harden completely, and your Angelica archangelica herbal cream is ready to use. When using the cream, apply it to your skin, such as your wrists, temples, or behind your ears, to help alleviate symptoms of altitude sickness like headaches and nausea.
remedies.image_alt: Panax ginseng herbal creams for altitude sickness

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Flavonoids are good for altitude sickness because they help reduce inflammation and improve blood flow to the brain, which can help alleviate symptoms such as headaches and fatigue that occur when the body cannot adapt to high elevations.
Saponins are good for altitude sickness because they help to improve blood flow to the brain and reduce inflammation in the body, making it easier for people to adapt to high altitudes.

Polysaccharides are good for altitude sickness because they help to reduce inflammation and improve blood flow to the brain, which can help alleviate symptoms such as headaches and dizziness that occur when the body is exposed to high altitudes.
Triterpenoids are good for altitude sickness because they help to reduce inflammation and improve blood circulation, which can help the body adapt to the lower oxygen levels found at high altitudes.

Phenolic acids are good for altitude sickness because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and fluid buildup in the lungs and brain, which are common symptoms of altitude sickness.

remedies.remedy_desc: Astragalus membranaceus cream is used to help people who go to high places and get altitude sickness. Altitude sickness is a problem when the body gets too little oxygen at high elevations. Astragalus membranaceus has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for a long time. It is said to help the body adapt to high altitudes by increasing the amount of red blood cells, which carry oxygen to the body's different parts. This can help reduce the symptoms of altitude sickness, such as headaches, nausea, and fatigue. The cream may also help to reduce inflammation and promote healing in the body, which can be helpful when people are sick. Some people also use Astragalus membranaceus cream to boost their immune system, which can help fight off infections that can be caused by altitude sickness.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Angelica archangelica herbal cream that helps with altitude sickness, you will need fresh or dried Angelica roots, a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil, and a few other ingredients. First, dry the Angelica roots if they are fresh by tying them in small bunches and hanging them upside down in a warm, dark place. After they are dry, chop them finely and combine them with a carrier oil in a clean glass jar. Let the mixture steep in a cool, dark place for about two weeks, shaking the jar every day to help the roots release their healing properties into the oil. Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, pressing on the solids to get as much oil out as possible. Next, add a few tablespoons of beeswax and a few drops of essential oil to the oil, and heat the mixture gently in a double boiler until the beeswax has melted. Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool and solidify, then transfer the herbal cream to a clean glass jar and store it in the fridge. When you need it, apply a small amount to your skin, paying special attention to areas that are prone to altitude sickness.
remedies.image_alt: Astragalus membranaceus herbal creams for altitude sickness

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Quinine is good for altitude sickness because it helps reduce inflammation and pain caused by high altitude, and it also acts as a natural diuretic to help the body get rid of excess fluid and salt.
Quinidine is good for altitude sickness because it helps to reduce inflammation and improve blood flow to the brain, making it easier for the body to adapt to high elevations.

Quinovic acid glycosides are good for altitude sickness because they help to reduce inflammation and improve blood flow to the brain, which can help to alleviate the symptoms of altitude sickness such as headaches and nausea.

Alkaloids in Angelica Archangelica are good for altitude sickness because they have been shown to help improve blood circulation and reduce inflammation in the body, which can help alleviate the symptoms of altitude sickness such as headaches and fatigue.
Tannins are good for altitude sickness because they help reduce inflammation and improve blood flow in the body, which can ease the symptoms of altitude sickness such as headaches and fatigue.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinchona officinalis cream is used to help prevent and treat altitude sickness. This condition happens when people travel to high places and their bodies can't get enough oxygen. The cream is made from the bark of the Cinchona tree, which contains a chemical called quinine. Quinine helps to thin the blood and make it easier for oxygen to get to the body's cells. When you apply the cream to your skin, the quinine is absorbed into your bloodstream, where it can start working to prevent altitude sickness. The cream also helps to reduce inflammation and swelling in the body, which can be caused by altitude sickness. By using Cinchona officinalis cream, people who live or travel to high places can reduce their risk of getting altitude sickness and make their bodies better able to handle the lack of oxygen.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Angelica archangelica herbal creams for altitude sickness, start by gathering the necessary ingredients and equipment. You'll need dried Angelica root, a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil, beeswax, vitamin E oil, and a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water. Begin by infusing the Angelica root in the carrier oil for several hours or overnight, allowing the plant's properties to extract into the oil. Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean bowl, discarding the solids. Next, melt the beeswax in the double boiler or heat-proof bowl, then slowly add the infused oil and stir until fully combined. Finally, add a few drops of vitamin E oil and stir well, allowing the mixture to cool and thicken into a smooth, rich cream. Once set, transfer the cream to a clean container and store it in the refrigerator. When needed, apply a small amount of the cream to the skin, ideally on the wrists, temples, or behind the ears, to help alleviate symptoms of altitude sickness, such as dizziness and nausea.
remedies.image_alt: Cinchona officinalis herbal creams for altitude sickness

remedies.remedy_desc: Rhodiola rosea creams can be helpful in reducing the effects of altitude sickness. Altitude sickness happens when the body can't get enough oxygen at high elevations. This causes problems like headaches, fatigue, and nausea. Rhodiola rosea creams contain active ingredients that help the body adapt to high altitudes. The cream works by reducing the production of stress hormones, like cortisol, which can worsen altitude sickness symptoms. It also helps increase the production of oxygen-carrying red blood cells, which improves oxygen delivery to the body's tissues. Additionally, Rhodiola rosea creams have anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling and pain caused by altitude sickness. As a result, people who use Rhodiola rosea creams may experience relief from altitude sickness symptoms, such as headaches and fatigue. This allows them to continue their activities without feeling too unwell.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Angelica archangelica herbal creams that help with altitude sickness, start by harvesting or purchasing dried Angelica root. Clean and dry the root, then chop it into small pieces. Combine the chopped root with a carrier oil, such as coconut or olive oil, in a small saucepan. Heat the mixture over low heat, stirring occasionally, for about 30 minutes to allow the root's active ingredients to infuse into the oil. Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container, discarding the solids. Add a few drops of vitamin E oil and beeswax to the liquid, and heat the mixture again to melt the wax. Remove from heat and let it cool and thicken. Once the cream has cooled and thickened, transfer it to a glass jar and store it in the refrigerator. To use, apply a small amount to your skin before ascending to high altitudes, as the Angelica root has natural anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help alleviate symptoms of altitude sickness.

remedies.remedy_desc: Withania somnifera cream is a natural remedy that can help alleviate symptoms of altitude sickness. This is because it contains compounds that improve circulation and reduce inflammation in the body. When we go to high altitudes, our bodies struggle to get enough oxygen, leading to headaches, fatigue, and shortness of breath. Withania somnifera cream works by opening up blood vessels, allowing more oxygen to reach our organs and tissues. This can help reduce the severity of altitude sickness symptoms. Additionally, Withania somnifera has antioxidant properties that help protect our cells from damage caused by high-altitude conditions. It also has adaptogenic properties, which help our bodies adapt to the stress of high altitude. By using Withania somnifera cream, people who travel to high-altitude areas can potentially reduce their risk of developing altitude sickness and enjoy their trip with less discomfort.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Angelica archangelica herbal creams that help with altitude sickness, you'll need dried Angelica roots and a few simple ingredients. First, prepare a strong tea by steeping one teaspoon of dried Angelica roots in one cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes, then let it cool down. Next, combine the cooled tea with one tablespoon of beeswax, one tablespoon of coconut oil, and one tablespoon of shea butter in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water. Stir gently until the beeswax and oils have melted and mixed with the tea, creating a smooth and creamy consistency. Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool and thicken at room temperature. Once it has reached a thick, spreadable consistency, transfer the mixture to a clean container and store it in the refrigerator. When you need relief from altitude sickness, apply a small amount of the Angelica cream to your skin, focusing on areas like your wrists, temples, and behind your ears, where the blood vessels are closest to the surface. The Angelica archangelica properties in the cream may help to improve circulation and alleviate symptoms of altitude sickness.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea cream is believed to help with altitude sickness due to its potential anti-inflammatory properties. At high elevations, the body's oxygen levels decrease, which can cause swelling and pain in the body. Echinacea purpurea has been traditionally used to reduce swelling and ease pain. Its cream form may help to directly apply these benefits to the affected areas, such as the hands and feet, which often swell at high altitudes. Additionally, Echinacea purpurea may help to boost the immune system, which can be weakened by the change in environment and lower oxygen levels. Some people also use it to help with fatigue and headaches, which are common symptoms of altitude sickness. By using Echinacea purpurea cream, some individuals may be able to alleviate these symptoms and feel more comfortable at high elevations.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Angelica archangelica herbal cream that helps with altitude sickness, start by gathering dried Angelica root and flowers, which can be purchased from a health food store or grown in your garden. You'll also need a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil, beeswax, and a double boiler. Combine 2 tablespoons of dried Angelica root and flowers with 8 ounces of carrier oil in a heat-proof jar, and let it sit in the sun for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar daily. This process is called maceration, and it allows the herbs to infuse into the oil. After the maceration period, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another container, discarding the solids. Next, melt 2 tablespoons of beeswax in the double boiler, then slowly add the infused oil, stirring constantly. Continue stirring until the mixture thickens and cools, forming a creamy texture. Transfer the cream to a clean jar and label it. When you're at high altitude, apply a small amount of the cream to your skin, ideally on your pulse points, to help ease symptoms of altitude sickness, such as headaches and nausea.

remedies.remedy_desc: Angelica archangelica cream is a natural remedy that may help with altitude sickness. The cream comes from the root of the Angelica plant, which contains a compound called angelica root extract. This extract has been used for centuries to help with various health issues, including altitude sickness. When you're at high altitudes, your body has less oxygen, which can cause headaches, nausea, and fatigue. The angelica root extract in the cream may help reduce inflammation and improve blood flow to the brain, which can help alleviate these symptoms. Additionally, the cream may also help to calm the digestive system, which can become upset due to the lower oxygen levels at high altitudes. Some people have reported that using Angelica archangelica cream has helped them feel better and recover faster from altitude sickness.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Angelica archangelica herbal cream for altitude sickness, start by drying the Angelica roots and leaves in a low-temperature oven or by air-drying them. This will help preserve the herb's properties. Once dry, grind the Angelica into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or a mortar and pestle. Combine the powder with a carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil in a ratio of about 1 part Angelica to 2 parts oil. Heat the mixture gently in a double boiler or a saucepan over low heat, stirring occasionally, until the powder dissolves into the oil. Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool. Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a bowl to separate the oil from any remaining solids. Transfer the resulting herbal cream to a glass jar and store it in a cool, dark place. To use, apply a small amount of the cream to the skin, usually on the wrists or temples, before ascending to high altitudes, as the Angelica's properties may help alleviate symptoms of altitude sickness.
remedies.image_alt: Angelica archangelica herbal creams for altitude sickness

intro_desc: Altitude sickness is a serious issue that can occur when people travel to high-altitude areas. At high elevations, the air is thinner, and the body has to work harder to get enough oxygen. If not addressed, altitude sickness can lead to headaches, fatigue, and even more serious health problems. One way to alleviate symptoms is by using herbal creams.  Herbal creams with Ginkgo biloba and Zingiber officinale can be beneficial in addressing altitude sickness. Ginkgo biloba is known for its ability to improve blood flow and increase oxygen delivery to the brain. When you're at high altitude, your brain and body need more oxygen. Ginkgo biloba can help your body adapt to the lower air pressure. Zingiber officinale, or ginger, has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce pain and discomfort associated with altitude sickness.  If you're traveling to high-altitude areas, it's essential to take preventive measures to avoid altitude sickness. Using herbal creams with Ginkgo biloba and Zingiber officinale can help alleviate symptoms and make your trip more enjoyable. By taking care of your body, you can improve your quality of life and make the most out of your travels. Fixing altitude sickness can prevent you from experiencing debilitating headaches and fatigue, allowing you to focus on the things you love, whether it's hiking, exploring, or simply enjoying the scenery. By taking care of your body, you can improve your overall well-being and enjoy your travels without any discomfort.
supplementary_best: A combination of herbal creams that may help remedy altitude sickness is a blend of Ginkgo biloba, Astragalus membranaceus, and Rhodiola rosea. Ginkgo biloba is chosen for its ability to improve blood flow and oxygen delivery to the body's tissues, which is compromised at high altitudes. Astragalus membranaceus is added for its adaptogenic properties, helping the body to adjust to the new environment and reducing stress. Rhodiola rosea is included for its ability to increase endurance and reduce fatigue, both common symptoms of altitude sickness. This combination is effective because it addresses multiple aspects of altitude sickness, improving circulation, reducing stress, and increasing the body's ability to cope with the new environment. The three herbs work together to help alleviate the symptoms of altitude sickness, making it a potentially beneficial combination for individuals experiencing altitude sickness.
supplementary_avoid: If you have altitude sickness, it's best to avoid herbal creams containing Ephedra sinica. This herb can increase heart rate and blood pressure, making altitude sickness worse. Ephedra sinica can also cause dehydration, which is already a concern at high altitudes due to low air pressure and dry air. Additionally, some herbal creams may contain Ephedra sinica in combination with other stimulants, such as Kola nut (Cola nitida), which can further exacerbate the symptoms of altitude sickness. Other herbal creams to be cautious with include those containing Ashwagandha (Withania somnifera-free) alternative herbs like  'Silybum marianum', but more specifically 'Avena sativa',  'Passiflora incarnata',  and 'Valeriana officinalis'. These herbs can cause drowsiness and interact with medications used to treat altitude sickness.
supplementary_other_remedies: At high altitudes, the body struggles to get enough oxygen. Natural remedies can help alleviate altitude sickness symptoms. Consuming ginger can be beneficial as it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce nausea and headaches. Ginger also improves digestion, which can be affected at high altitudes. Drinking plenty of water is also essential, but some people find that electrolyte-rich beverages like coconut water or sports drinks are more effective at replenishing lost salts and minerals. Another natural remedy is consuming foods rich in antioxidants, such as dark chocolate and berries, which help protect the body from oxidative stress caused by high altitude. Some people also use saltwater inhalation to relieve congestion and sinus pressure, but this method is not well-studied. Drinking warm tea, like peppermint or chamomile, can also provide relief from altitude sickness symptoms, as it helps to calm the mind and body.
supplementary_related_ailments: People who have altitude sickness often experience headaches, nausea, and fatigue. These symptoms occur because the body struggles to adapt to lower oxygen levels at high elevations. Medicinal herbs can help alleviate these symptoms. For example, feverfew and ginger can reduce headache pain due to their anti-inflammatory properties. Feverfew contains a compound called parthenolide, which helps block pain-causing chemicals. Ginger's active compound, gingerol, has anti-inflammatory properties that help ease pain and reduce inflammation in the body.  Another common symptom of altitude sickness is nausea and vomiting. Peppermint and chamomile can help calm the stomach and reduce nausea. Peppermint's menthol content helps soothe the stomach and reduce inflammation, while chamomile's apigenin content has a calming effect on the body. This can help alleviate nausea and vomiting associated with altitude sickness.  Fatigue is also a common symptom of altitude sickness. Ashwagandha and ginseng are herbs that can help increase energy levels and reduce fatigue. Ashwagandha's adaptogenic properties help the body adapt to stress, which can contribute to fatigue. Ginseng's active compound, ginsenoside, can increase energy levels and reduce fatigue by improving blood flow and oxygen delivery to the body's cells.  These herbs work by reducing inflammation, improving blood flow, and increasing oxygen delivery to the body's cells. They can help alleviate symptoms of altitude sickness, such as headaches, nausea, and fatigue, by supporting the body's natural response to stress and promoting overall well-being.
